assertionSummary: Forbid actual TypeScript import declarations from `/base-standard/` outside `packages/civ7-adapter`.
implementationOutcome: deleted the stale command packet; retained authority is `enforce_adapter_only_base_standard_imports`
notes: The existing Grit rule is narrower and structural; it intentionally does not flag plain provenance strings.

assertionSummary: Broad shell `rg` scan flags any `/base-standard/` string outside the adapter, including map-policy provenance/source labels.
implementationOutcome: deleted with the command packet; not preserved as Habitat runtime-import authority
proofCommands: bun tools/habitat/bin/dev.ts check --rule block_unapproved_base_standard_boundary_leaks --json -> selector-validation failure after deletion
notes: Preflight red was caused by provenance strings, not runtime imports. Keeping this branch would preserve the wrong oracle.

assertionSummary: First non-empty line in canonical MapGen docs must start with `<toc>`.
implementationOutcome: not implemented in this wave; source-shape branch is separable but current rule remains command-check
notes: A future Markdown Grit split can own this branch without touching filesystem target resolution.

assertionSummary: Non-router canonical MapGen docs must contain a `## Ground truth anchors` section.
implementationOutcome: not implemented in this wave; source-shape branch is separable but current rule remains command-check
notes: Do not combine this with link target existence in one Grit pattern.

assertionSummary: Backticked file path anchors and legacy-router targets must resolve to existing repo paths.
implementationOutcome: retained in command-check; current focused proof still fails on pre-existing missing anchor targets
proofCommands: bun tools/habitat/bin/dev.ts check --rule validate_mapgen_docs_anchors_and_references --json -> fail, 12 errors / 3 warnings
notes: Filesystem target resolution is validator behavior, not Grit source matching.

assertionSummary: `@mapgen/*` mentions are warnings by default and errors only under `--forbid-workspace-aliases`.
implementationOutcome: retained in command-check; do not convert warning-policy flags to enforced Grit errors
proofCommands: bun tools/habitat/bin/dev.ts check --rule validate_mapgen_docs_anchors_and_references --json -> fail, warnings included
notes: Severity policy and CLI flags are part of the validator surface.

assertionSummary: Local glossary-like term definitions are warnings by default and errors only under `--strict-terms`.
implementationOutcome: retained in command-check; do not convert strictness flags to unconditional Grit errors
proofCommands: bun tools/habitat/bin/dev.ts check --rule validate_mapgen_docs_anchors_and_references --json -> fail, warnings included
